name: Stellenbosch CS Website
description: Winning entry in Stellenbosch University Developers Society's 2026 Div and Conquer Hackathon. A complete redesign for the Computer Science department.

caseStudy.summary: A hackathon redesign that became a full modern department platform, combining strong visual direction, multilingual support, SEO foundations, and production-ready CMS integration.

content.text: This project started in a Stellenbosch University Developer's Society hackathon to redesign the Computer Science Department's outdated website. The goal was to create a modern, mobile-friendly experience that felt credible, clear, and useful to prospective students, current students, staff, and alumni.
Design and build process

I started in Figma to shape the UI direction and created a custom hero video loop using Stellenbosch reference imagery and AI-generated clips. Once that visual system was established, I built the platform in Next.js and Tailwind with ShadCN UI, which made it easy to keep components consistent while still customising the experience.

content.alt: Figma UI planning and concept exploration
content.caption: Early UI planning and design direction work.
Final hero section with cinematic background loop and glassmorphism styling.

content.text: Tech stack and architecture

items: Framework: Next.js 16
Styling: Tailwind CSS
Component system: ShadCN UI
Internationalisation: next-intl (English, Afrikaans, isiXhosa)
Content layer: Drupal JSON:API integration prepared

content.text: Scope delivered

items: Home, About, Research, Programmes, Contact, staff pages, and alumni/student information sections.
Undergraduate and postgraduate programme route structures.
Mobile-optimised layouts and transitions across all key templates.

content.text: Highlights

items: Glassmorphism UI system for consistent, modern visual identity.
Smooth section and page transitions for polished UX.
Full locale-aware routing and translation support for three languages.
SEO groundwork including sitemap, robots, and metadata strategy.
Interactive terminal easter egg with command simulation and CS-themed content.

content.caption: The hidden terminal experience designed for CS students.

content.text: Headless CMS status

Drupal is configured as a headless CMS via JSON:API, and the integration layer for pulling module content is in place. During the hackathon timeline, content entry and multilingual Drupal management were the main bottlenecks, so translations currently remain in JSON files. The architecture is ready to switch fully to dynamic Drupal-backed content once data population is complete.
This was one of those projects where getting the design direction right up front made the entire build faster and more cohesive.

name: Coco Blue Website
description: A full e-commerce platform for Coco Blue, designed and built from scratch across frontend and backend.

caseStudy.summary: A long-running, founder-led e-commerce build that started in December 2024 and grew into a full platform across storefront and backend operations.

content.text: I spent two months of my December 2024 holiday building the first major version of the Coco Blue platform. Since then, I have kept developing it and I am proud of how much of a full-scale project it has become.

Coco Blue needed more than a storefront. The goal was to create a platform that could handle branded product discovery, ordering, and day-to-day management workflows without constant technical overhead.
Core outcomes

items: Introduced a structured product browsing and checkout flow.
Created backend APIs for inventory and order workflows.
Improved trust signals with stronger product and policy presentation.

content.caption: Coco Blue hero section presenting the brand and primary shopping journey.
Necklaces catalog page focused on clean product browsing and mobile-first shopping.

content.alt: Coco Blue backend categories management dashboard
content.caption: Categories management interface in the backend dashboard.

content.text: What comes next
The next phase is evolving the backend into a multi-tenant platform so it can serve more South African businesses, with stronger operational tooling and scalable internal workflows.

name: Ticket Brew
description: A modern South African ticketing platform that gives organisers control over branding, fees, and operations.

caseStudy.summary: A launch-stage startup case study on fee control, mobile-first ticketing, and event operations for South African organisers.

content.text: Ticket Brew was built by an organiser who needed elegant digital tickets, branded event pages, and a checkout flow people would actually complete on mobile. The company is newly established and preparing for launch, with the product focused on practical tools for South African events.
What the product is built around

items: Pricing control from 0% to 100%, so organisers choose how checkout fees are split with buyers.
Branded event pages that reflect the event identity instead of generic marketplace templates.
Mobile-first checkout and WhatsApp ticket delivery for paid orders.
Fast door scanning, promoter tracking, and local South African payouts.

content.text: Current stage
Ticket Brew is in launch rollout. The immediate focus is polishing onboarding, validating early organiser workflows, and scaling reliable operations as the first wave of events goes live.
Why this case study matters

items: It shows how a real organiser pain point became a focused product brief.
It demonstrates a clear differentiation strategy against fixed-fee incumbents.
It documents early company-building decisions while transitioning from build mode to launch.

name: QR Quest
description: An MVP web app for creating interactive scavenger hunts using QR-driven game loops.

caseStudy.summary: A playful MVP focused on engagement loops and easy game authoring.

content.text: QR Quest was built to test whether non-technical users could create engaging scavenger hunt experiences without custom development.
MVP scope

items: Create clues and route checkpoints.
Generate QR codes for physical locations.
Track progress and completion states.

name: Angel Paws Website
description: A nonprofit website for Angel Paws Therapy Dogs with clearer storytelling and support pathways.

caseStudy.summary: A mission-driven site refresh focused on trust and donation-readiness.

content.text: For Angel Paws, clarity and empathy were key. The site had to explain impact quickly and make it easy for visitors to learn, volunteer, and contribute.

name: Gift Redeem Website
description: A creative seasonal web app for turning holiday gifts into interactive reveal experiences.

caseStudy.summary: A fun personal build combining narrative and simple interaction mechanics.

content.text: This experiment explored how small product touches can make a simple gift exchange feel more memorable and personal.

name: New Year Bot
description: A Discord bot with celebration-focused features including countdown and community event interactions.

caseStudy.summary: An early community bot project that grew through iterative feature releases.

content.text: This bot was a practical way to learn API integrations, command systems, and long-lived event handling in Discord communities.

name: Portfolio Website
description: The personal website for showcasing projects, writing, and ongoing experiments.

caseStudy.summary: A continuously evolving personal platform and technical playground.

content.text: This site is where design direction, frontend architecture, and writing style all converge. Every iteration becomes a testing ground for the next one.
